options.text: sais
connais
ignore

exercises.explanation: A skill (+ infinitive) → savoir: je sais jouer.

options.text: connais
sais
es conscient

exercises.explanation: A person/noun → connaître: tu connais ce professeur.

exercises.template: J'{{b1}} à quelle heure commence le cours.
exercises.explanation: Ignorer = to NOT know (not 'to ignore'): j'ignore à quelle heure…
Être doué EN maths / POUR les langues — pour before a field of activity.

exercises.prompt: « Il a la bosse des maths » means…

options.text: He has a real head for figures
He failed his maths exam
He hurt his head in maths class

exercises.explanation: Avoir la bosse des maths = to be naturally gifted at maths.

exercises.template: Je {{b1}} que l'examen est en juin.
exercises.explanation: Facts (que-clauses) take savoir: je sais que…

exercises.prompt: Match each talent idiom to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "She underestimates her own talent."
Listen to the teacher. What does she say about Léa?
exercises.transcript: Léa a des facilités en langues et sait déjà lire des partitions — elle a l'oreille musicale.

options.text: Languages come easily to her and she has a musical ear
She struggles with languages but loves music
She knows the music teacher personally

exercises.explanation: des facilités en langues + l'oreille musicale — two talent idioms in one report.

exercises.prompt: Read the school report extract and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Thomas est habile de ses mains et fait preuve d'une grande capacité de concentration en atelier. En revanche, il méconnaît ses aptitudes en expression écrite : il ignore qu'il a de réelles dispositions. Nous sommes conscients de son potentiel et souhaitons qu'il en prenne conscience lui aussi.
exercises.passageEn: Thomas is good with his hands and shows great concentration in the workshop. On the other hand, he underestimates his aptitude for writing: he doesn't know that he has real ability. We are aware of his potential and hope he becomes aware of it too.

options.text: He is good with his hands and focused in the workshop
He is the best writer in class
He has a musical ear

questions.prompt: « Il ignore qu'il a de réelles dispositions » means…

options.text: He doesn't know he has real ability
He ignores his teachers on purpose
He refuses to write
Je connais que tu as raison.
Je sais que tu as raison.
Je connais ton quartier.

exercises.explanation: « Connaître que » doesn't exist — facts take savoir: je sais que…

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Je sais conduire, mais je ne connais pas cette route.

options.text: I know how to drive, but I don't know this road.
Have you heard about the high-school reform?
I don't know what time the lecture starts.
to know
to not know
to be in the know about

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.

pairs.right: to have a head for figures

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order: “I don't know what time the lecture starts.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Je sais conduire, mais je ne connais pas cette route.
exercises.targetEn: I know how to drive, but I don't know this road.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: J'ignore à quelle heure commence la conférence.
exercises.targetEn: I don't know what time the lecture starts.
